areas.label: Economy & money
areas.synthesis: Moody's lowered the sovereign rating one notch, from Baa2 to Baa3, and changed the outlook from negative to stable. Mexico remains investment grade.

headlines.title: Moody's lowers Mexico's sovereign rating to Baa3; outlook stable
Annual inflation falls to 3.37% in June
Banxico keeps the policy rate at 6.50%

areas.label: Payments
areas.synthesis: The deal would give Equifax 100% of Círculo de Crédito and is expected to close in the fourth quarter, subject to regulatory approval.

headlines.title: Equifax agrees to buy Círculo de Crédito for a $750M enterprise value

areas.label: Trade
areas.synthesis: The agreement stays in force, and the three countries will review it every year until they extend it or the process reaches 2036. Mexico and the United States have another negotiating round the week of July 20.

headlines.title: USMCA stays in force, but the U.S. does not extend it at the first review.

areas.label: Politics
areas.synthesis: Pending federal and local judicial elections move from 2027 to 2028, and the Supreme Court's structure changes. A separate decree adds foreign interference as grounds to void an election.

headlines.title: Second judicial reform published in the DOF: judicial elections pushed to 2028, Supreme Court restructured

areas.label: Society & security
areas.synthesis: The government says the daily average fell from 86.9 victims in September 2024 to 47.3 in May 2026. These are preliminary government figures; the formal monthly SESNSP release is separate.

headlines.title: Government reports the daily homicide average down 46% since September 2024

areas.label: U.S.–Mexico
areas.synthesis: Mexico says it will seek criminal complaints over 17 deaths connected to ICE, 14 in custody and three during operations. It will also pursue civil action against private detention operators.

headlines.title: Mexico to file complaints over deaths connected to U.S. immigration enforcement
Temporary 10% U.S. surcharge on non-USMCA goods runs through July 24
May U.S. goods deficit with Mexico widens to $20.1B
